text:
<think>I should first calculate how many gifts the first two friends want in total.</think>
The first two friends want 5 gifts each, so together they want 10 gifts.
<think>Now I need to calculate how many gifts the other three friends want in total.</think>
The other three friends want 2 gifts each, so together they want 6 gifts.
<think>I should now add the gifts for all five friends to get the subtotal before the extra gifts.</think>
Adding these together, Cherrie buys 10 + 6 = 16 gifts for her friends.
<think>Next, I need to include the 10 additional random gifts Cherrie buys.</think>
Cherrie also buys 10 more random gifts.
<think>Now I should sum the subtotal and the extra gifts to get the final total.</think>
In total, Cherrie buys 16 + 10 = 26 gifts.
<think>I should now present the final answer after the required #### marker.</think>
#### 26
